title: Nano-island-encapsulated cobalt single-atom catalysts for breaking activity-stability trade-off in Fenton-like reactions

nodes.evidence: Single-atom catalysts (SACs) have been increasingly acknowledged
SACs face a trade-off between activity and stability in peroxymonosulfate (PMS)-based systems
enhance the activity and stability of PMS activation via an 'island-sea' synergistic effect
Characterized by minimal leaching of active sites
achieve near-complete PMS conversion to ¹O₂
high reactivity stems from unsaturated coordination environments
migration and aggregation of metal atoms
neutral microenvironment within the reaction system
remarkable selectivity in exclusively generating sulfate radicals
standard reduction potential ranging from 2.5 to 3.1 V
cerium oxide nano-islands containing oxygen vacancies (CeO₂₋ₓ)
carbon materials commonly act as supports
complete removal of various recalcitrant pollutants
Persistent organic pollutants in wastewater present substantial risks

title: High-entropy alloy enables multi-path electron synergism and lattice oxygen activation for enhanced oxygen evolution activity

nodes.evidence: Electrocatalytic oxygen evolution reaction (OER) is key to several energy technologies
Leveraging the lattice oxygen activation mechanism (LOM) is a strategy for boosting its activity
We reveal that high-entropy alloys (HEAs) can efficiently activate the LOM
at a current density of 10 mA cm⁻²
We analyzed the catalyst surface using Raman spectroscopy
in both KOH and TMAOH solutions
peak observed at 506 cm⁻¹ in the Raman spectrum is indicative of the A1g stretching mode
interaction between TMA⁺ ions and the negatively charged oxygen-containing intermediates (O₂⁻)
evaluation of the key intermediate μ-OO
in both KOH and TMAOH solutions
transformation from hydrated nickel hydroxide to a structure resembling nickel oxide
a pair of peaks at 560 and 680 cm⁻¹, which correspond to the Fe–O bond
three distinct peaks at 458, 755, and 952 cm⁻¹ is characteristic of TMA⁺ cations
higher availability of binding sites for TMA⁺
high-entropy structure promotes the initiation of an efficient electrocatalytic LOM pathway
deep oxidation of the active Ni sites

achieves overpotential of 220 mV at 10 mA cm⁻²

title: MXene-Assisted NiFe sulfides for high-performance anion exchange membrane seawater electrolysis

nodes.evidence: electron distribution to trigger lattice oxygen mechanism
high OER activity (1.598 V@2 A cm-2)
energy consumption of 48.4 kWh kg-1 H2

metadata.evidence: integrating MXene (Ti3C2) with NiFe sulfides ((Ni,Fe)S2@Ti3C2)
strong interaction between (Ni,Fe)S2 and Ti3C2
induces electron distribution to trigger lattice oxygen mechanism
prohibits the dissolution of Fe species via Ti-O-Fe bonding
abundant groups of Ti3C2 provide effective Cl- resistance
trigger lattice oxygen mechanism improving intrinsic activity
lower ΔECl indicates weaker Cl- interaction
analyzed via electrochemical impedance spectroscopy
multilevel optimization of anodic catalyst
influence of pore size of gas diffusion layer on electrolyzer performance

metadata.claim: 化学学科中电子分布调控可触发晶格氧机制，但需材料科学的多级催化剂优化实现稳定活性
晶格氧机制提升析氧反应活性需协同材料科学的气体扩散层结构设计
高活性下维持抗氯离子腐蚀能力需材料科学的多尺度界面调控
化学学科中与硫化物复合可增强电子传递，但需材料科学优化异质界面稳定性
硫化物-电子分布协同作用需材料科学的原位表征技术验证动态过程
电子分布触发晶格氧机制的瓶颈需材料科学的理论模拟辅助机制解析
化学学科中金属氧键抑制溶解的稳定性机制需材料科学的多孔结构设计强化
长期稳定性提升需材料科学的气体扩散层孔径调控协同界面保护
工业电流密度下的稳定性瓶颈需材料科学的梯度电极结构突破
